data.name: Character Name
data.description: Primary description: who this character is, their role, their expertise (if an assistant), and key personality traits. 150-400 words usually. For a domain-expert assistant, this is where you establish what they know and how they frame their help. For a roleplay character, this is the richest description of the entity.
data.personality: Speech patterns, quirks, tone. How they respond to users. If they're sarcastic, caring, blunt, flowery — say it here with examples. 50-200 words. Avoid MBTI/astrology alone; give concrete behavioral cues the model can mimic.
data.scenario: The setting or framing for interactions. For an assistant: 'User has just opened the app and needs help with X.' For a roleplay: the opening situation. Can be short — 1-3 sentences.
data.first_mes: The character's opening message. Make it compelling — this is what the user sees first. For an assistant, list what they can help with. For a roleplay, establish voice and set the scene. Should showcase the personality.
data.mes_example:
<START>
{{user}}: Example user line
{{char}}: Example character response that demonstrates voice, formatting preferences, and response length.
<START>
{{user}}: Second example
{{char}}: Second response.
data.creator_notes: Internal notes for other people who might import this card. Not sent to the model.
data.creator: Your name

extensions.backstory: Optional deeper backstory. Not always sent to the model depending on preset.
extensions.appearance: Physical description — important for selfie generation and sprite/image generation. Be concrete: height, build, hair, eye color, typical clothing.
